content_body:
Given that history tends to repeat itself and older trends continually come back into style, here are the best retro activities in Blue Ridge! Whether children or adults, bring out your inner kid and re-discover the wonder and thrill of vintage experiences!

2nd Story Entertainment:

An antique mall renovated into an indoor mini golf center, 2nd Story Entertainment holds a maze of rooms and is fun for the whole family! After your round of mini golf, check out the arcade machines, checkers, and dartboard!

Swan Drive-In:

One of three drive-ins left in Georgia, The Swan Drive-In Theater is a favorite summer activity by all! Bring some blankets and pillows, and turn your SUV into a comfy fort while enjoying the latest movie releases. Their concessions specialize in funnel cakes and deep-fried Oreos, so enjoy a date night or family adventure at the movies!

Deaf Man Vinyl:

Located in the nearby town of McCaysville, GA, Deaf Man Vinyl boasts various vinyl records and accessories for all your favorite rock bands! Find your favorite album, get a sticker to commemorate your music taste, or get a hoodie to make a fashion statement.

Vertigo Pinball:

For some excitement for all ages, visit Vertigo Pinball! Engage in some exciting competition on your favorite pinball tables (ranging from the 1990s to the present) while sipping on a beer or two. For more pinball history, visit the Georgia Pinball Museum to play record and see pinball machines from the 1930s to 1990s! 

Huck's General Store:

A staple of Blue Ridge, Huck's allows you to treat yourself to your favorite old-fashioned candy! You can also find some vintage Blue Ridge accessories, retro games, and toys for the little ones in your group.
title: Retro Renegade: Nostalgic Things to Do in Blue Ridge, GA
